Curriculum Vitae 
Personal information 
• First name(s) / Surname(s): Daniel Muamba 
• Address(es): Residing in Goodwood, Cape Town 7460 
• Mobile phone: +27 67 639 2835 
• E-mail: eyeofdaniel@gmail.com 
• Gender: Male 
• Nationality: Congolese 
• Date of birth: 25/05/2003 
• Availability: Immediately 
• Expectation: Negotiable 
Work experience 
• Occupation and Date: Runner/Waiter (27 June 2018 – 20 July 2018) 
o Main activities and responsibilities: Greet & serve guests and take orders. 
Routinely cleaned work areas, immediately reported accidents, injuries or unsafe 
work conditions to manager. Provided friendly and attentive service. Grilled and 
helped as a cashier. 
o Name and address of employer: Senhor Calistos Portuguese Restaurant 
• Occupation and Date: Barista (12 December 2018 – 16 January 2018) 
o Main activities and responsibilities: Prepare and serve cold beverages. Clean 
and maintain clean workstation. Provide Customers with detailed menu. 
o Name and address of employer: Spearhead Bartenders Events Company. 
Contact: Joel, Manager, 064 279 4787. 
• Occupation and Date: Assistant Clerical worker (27 June 2019 – 20 July 2021) 
o Main activities and responsibilities: Data capturing (admin duties) 
o Name and address of employer: Ashanti. Contact: Mrs. Gina, 083 694 8458. 
• Occupation and Date: Sales Associate (5 March 2022 - 31 October 2022) 
o Main activities and responsibilities: I help customers with their design projects 
by interacting with them, understanding their needs, offering design tips and 
product suggestions, and creating personalized room designs. I also stay updated 
on products, handle transactions and work with the store team to meet sales goals. 
o Name and address of employer: @Homelivingspace. Manager: Dalina 072 248 
0262/  Floor Manager Uthicus: 084 926 1000 
• Occupation and Date: IT support technician (10 Jan 2023 – 30 Nov 2024) 
o Main activities and responsibilities: Integrating printing equipment, handling 
prepress systems and variable data printing, managing large files and color 
management. tools, ensuring software, troubleshooting the digital interfaces 
production machines. 
o Name and address of employer: Project Paper Congo, a.s.b.l. Contact: Mr Noel, 
0854 447 976. 
 

 
Education and training 
• Dates: 2021 (Implied by achievements like Valedictorian 2021 and Top 3 Matric 
candidate 2021) 
o Title of qualification awarded: Grade 12 
o Principal subjects/occupational skills covered: English, Afrikaans, 
Mathematics, Computer Application Technology, Accounting & Business Studies 
o Name and type of organisation providing education and training: Goodwood 
College 
• Dates: 2022 
o Title of qualification awarded: Total Barista - Professional Barista Course 
o Name and type of organisation providing education and training: Udemy 
Course. Instructor: Dale Robinson. Presented by: Winston Thomas. 
Personal skills 
• Mother tongue: Lingala 
• Other language(s): 
o English: Proficiency levels C2 (Proficient) 
o French: Proficiency levels: B1 (Intermediate). 
o Afrikaans: Proficiency levels: B1 (Intermediate). 
• Communication skills: 
o Good Communication Skills 
o Public speaking skills 
o Enjoys working with others as this improves confidence 
o Able to Work with People of Different Cultures 
o Has a good sense of humour 
• Organisational / managerial skills: 
o Time Management 
o Leadership skills 
o Interpersonal Skills 
o Team-building skills 
o Stress management 
o Self-reliant and goal orientated 
o Adaptable person yet very ambitious 
o Very focused & Self-disciplined 
o Discipline is a key focus for always working well under pressure 
o Has an active and dynamic approach to work and getting things done 
o Identifies and develops opportunities 
• Job-related skills: 
o Honest and Reliable 
o Hard worker 
o Willingness to learn 
o Enjoying being resourceful providing new ideas that could benefit the business 

o Motivated 
o Problem-solving 
o Analytic Thinking 
o Strategic Planning 
o Basic Accounting and Numeric skills 
o Has integrity and reliability, evident when counting money after church bake sales 
o Possesses the ability to handle multiple tasks every day 
o Extremely eager to learn and develop new skills as soon as possible 
o Dedicated, self-motivated, responsible 
o Has experience working in a team and individually 
• Digital competence: 
o Computer Literate 
o MS Word - Advanced 
o MS Excel - Advanced 
o MS PowerPoint - Advanced 
o MS Database - Advanced 
o E-mail Proficiency 
o Internet Proficiency 
o Researching Skills 
Additional information 
• Achievements: 
o Valedictorian 2021 
o Top 3 Matric candidate 2021 
o Prefect (Matric Council) 2021 
o Top 3 achiever 
o Computer Application Technology Student of the year 2021 
o Representative council of learners 
o Member of African Youth Congress 
o YCAP 2019 3rd place national Winners sponsored by Empowervate 
o Member of the 4IR research team sponsored by Sakhikamva Foundation and 
AYO 
• Awards / Certificates: 
o Highest in Computer Application Technology Award 
o Highest in Life Orientation Award 
o Certificate of merit for Diligence 
o Gold merit awards in Mathematics, Accounting, Business Studies, Economics, 
English HL, Creative Arts, and Life orientation 
o Dedication and consistency 
• Memberships / Participations: 
o Member of Africa Unite School Club 
o YCAP Empowerment 
o Member of African Youth Congress 
• Reason For Employment (Strengths): 
o My strengths are my dedication, self-motivation, reliability, and responsibility. 

o Experience working in a team and individually. 
o Extremely eager to learn and develop new skills as soon as possible. 
o Integrity and reliability are evident whenever I count money after our church bake 
sales. 
o Ability to handle multiple tasks every day. 
o Active and dynamic approach to work and getting things done. 
o Good sense of humor despite taking my job seriously. 
o Identifies and develops opportunities. 
• References: 
o Mrs. A. Esau, Acting Principal, Goodwood College, 021 534 6246, 
admin@goodwoodcollege.co.za 
o Mr. Turner, Grade 12 Subject Head and Prefect Coordinator, Goodwood College, 
+27 72 049 4296

sessions.title: New Session
sessions.source_text: The development of artificial intelligence brings significant ethical challenges. One of the main concerns is the lack of transparency in AI decision-making processes. Another issue is the potential for bias in algorithms, which can lead to unfair treatment of certain groups. Furthermore, the impact of AI on employment and privacy is a major topic of debate. Policymakers and technologists must work together to ensure that AI is developed and used responsibly.

scaffoldNodes.student_claim: Lack of transparency makes AI untrustworthy.

scaffoldNodes.title: Privacy Impact
scaffoldNodes.student_claim: AI poses significant risks to personal privacy.

scaffoldNodes.title: Academic Discourse
